steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 states Alvarez’s empirical finding: common pool resources can be sustainably managed by their users. Sentence 2 states that Alvarez’s work repudiates the “tragedy of the commons” — the theory that users will inevitably overexploit shared resources. The second sentence is a logical consequence of the first: if sustainable management is possible, then the theory predicting inevitable failure must be wrong. The blank must signal cause-and-effect or logical conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each option:
  • Choice A — By contrast,: signals that sentence 2 opposes sentence 1. But sentence 2 flows directly from sentence 1 — it does not present an opposing view. Violates the contrast rule. ✗
  • Choice B — For example,: signals that sentence 2 is an illustration of sentence 1. Repudiating a theory is a conclusion, not an example of empirically demonstrating sustainable management. Violates the exemplification rule. ✗
  • Choice C — That said,: signals a concession or caveat — acknowledging something but then qualifying it. Sentence 2 does not qualify sentence 1; it builds on it. Violates the concession rule. ✗
  • Choice D — As such,: means “consequently” or “therefore,” signaling that what follows is a logical result of what preceded. Alvarez’s evidence of sustainable management necessarily refutes the theory of inevitable overexploitation. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
